message:
fix(core): route extent allocation and setattr truncation through transaction scope for write consistency

Three critical write-path fixes for transactional correctness:

1. ext4_write: extent allocation now routes through TransactionBlockAdapter
   when a live transaction scope is present, ensuring that freshly allocated
   extent tree blocks are visible to subsequent writes within the same
   transaction. Re-acquires block_dev after allocation so bitmap reads see
   the new state. Previously all extent allocations went through the base
   device, causing phantom "block not found" errors on large sequential writes.

2. ext4_setattr: truncation (ftruncate) now reads the inode through scope
   (read_inode_with_scope) and routes truncate_extents + write_inode through
   TransactionBlockAdapter when a transaction is active. The setattr FsOps
   trait method now passes scope through instead of ignoring it with _scope.
   This fixes data loss where truncate freed extents but the inode update
   was invisible to subsequent operations in the same request.

3. ext4_write block_dev mutability: makes block_dev mutable in the write
   path to support re-acquisition after extent allocation.

Also adds tracing::error instrumentation to TransactionBlockAdapter
read_block/write_block for debugging staged-write visibility issues, adds
tracing-subscriber to ffs-harness dev-deps, and enables tracing in the
fuse_write_large_file E2E test.

- crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs: scope-aware extent allocation, scope-aware
  setattr, block_dev re-acquire, tracing instrumentation (+77/-8)
- crates/ffs-harness/Cargo.toml: add tracing-subscriber
- crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs: enable tracing in large-file test
- Cargo.lock: updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
